data.title: Thailand preparing crypto ETF guidelines to attract institutional investors amidst rising interest. #regulation #cryptocurrency
data.description: Thailand's Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is developing fresh regulations for cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and futures trading in efforts to establish the country as a leading crypto center for institutional investors in the region.

data.title: XRP metric signals potential 68% price plunge, driven by weak technicals and outflows. Can bulls protect key support?
data.description: The cost-basis metric for XRP has indicated a bearish signal, suggesting a potential significant price decrease in the near future. This downward trend is being driven by a weakening technical foundation and outflows from spot ETFs. The key question now is whether bullish investors will step in to protect critical price support levels amidst this challenging market environment.
Euro/Yen rises to historic high of 185.57.

data.title: I Squared Capital launches Radiant Energy Solutions under Middle East infrastructure strategy for mid-market investments.
data.description: In Abu Dhabi, a renowned global infrastructure investment manager, I Squared Capital, has introduced Radiant Energy Solutions as part of its Middle East infrastructure strategy. This investment aims to target mid-market opportunities in the region and is a testament to I Squared's dedication to building strong platforms over time. The strategy involves initiating with a core investment and progressively expanding to establish a leading market presence. This move signifies a significant development for I Squared's investment portfolio in the Middle East.

data.title: Institutional investors unlikely to boost Bitcoin to $150K, says researcher. Other analysts see them as essential for Bitcoin's rise.
data.description: Luke Gromen, the founder of FFTT, suggested that institutional investors might not significantly impact Bitcoin's price in the current year. On the contrary, other analysts have emphasized the crucial role these investors could play in driving a resurgence in Bitcoin value. It is essential to consider various perspectives on the potential influence of institutional investors on the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Nokia to release financial report for Q4 and full-year 2025 on January 29, 2026.
data.description: A recent study found that over 80% of Americans have a smartphone, with an average of 10 hours spent on it daily. This exceeds the global average of 3 hours. The study also revealed that nearly half of smartphone users check their devices within 5 minutes of waking up, and 75% do so within an hour. This high usage has raised concerns about its impact on mental health and productivity.

data.title: Chainlet halted due to $7M breach depegging Saga's stablecoin to $0.75. TVL plummeted by 55% in 24 hours.
data.description: The stablecoin linked to Saga in the US has decreased to $0.75, with the total value locked in the protocol dropping approximately 55% in the last day.

data.title: Essity's Capital Markets Day on May 7, 2026 in Gothenburg, Sweden with President Ulrika Kolsrud presenting strategy.
data.description: Essity, a hygiene and health company based in Stockholm, will host a Capital Markets Day on May 7, 2026, at its office in Gothenburg, Sweden. The event will feature President and CEO Ulrika Kolsrud, along with other members of the Executive Management Team, outlining Essity's strategic plans. This event will provide insight into the company's future direction for investors and stakeholders.

data.title: Opendoor CEO praises Trump's housing affordability efforts. Calls market unfair. Supports policy blocking investors.
data.description: Kaz Nejatian, CEO of Opendoor Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: OPEN), praised President Donald Trump for his efforts in addressing housing affordability. Nejatian commended recent policies aimed at tackling barriers that have hindered middle-class families from entering the single-family housing market. Speaking on The Pomp Podcast by Anthony Pompliano, Nejatian stated, "I think that President Trump is doing an excellent job," specifically highlighting the decision to block institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes. Nejatian emphasized the importance of ensuring that individuals like teachers can afford to buy homes on their salaries, asserting that this used to be possible and should be again. He acknowledged that the challenges faced by potential homebuyers stem from a complex system of barriers rather than just one factor. Nejatian described the obstacles as a "stack of stuff" that has made homeownership increasingly out of reach for many. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Buterin urges distributed validators to streamline Ethereum staking, aiming to enhance user experience for stakers.
data.description: The creator of Ethereum suggests that implementing distributed validator technology could enhance the staking process on the platform, despite its intricate setup. This technology has the potential to significantly improve Ethereum's staking experience by increasing efficiency and security.
